   452→## Code Examples
   453→
   454→Verified patterns from official sources:
   455→
   456→### BitsAndBytesConfig (4-bit NF4 QLoRA)
   457→```python
   458→# Source: https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/en/quantization/bitsandbytes
   459→# and TRL SFT docs: https://huggingface.co/docs/trl/en/sft_trainer
   460→import torch
   461→from transformers import BitsAndBytesConfig
   462→
   463→bnb_config = BitsAndBytesConfig(
   464→    load_in_4bit=True,
   465→    bnb_4bit_quant_type="nf4",          # NF4 = NormalFloat4, better than int4 for LLM weights
   466→    bnb_4bit_compute_dtype=torch.bfloat16,  # bfloat16 avoids NaN at 7B (float16 is unstable)
   467→    bnb_4bit_use_double_quant=True,      # double quantization reduces memory further
   468→)
   469→```
   470→
   471→### LoraConfig for CAUSAL_LM (vLLM-compatible)
   472→```python
   473→# Source: https://huggingface.co/docs/peft/package_reference/lora#peft.LoraConfig
   474→from peft import LoraConfig
   475→
   476→lora_config = LoraConfig(
   477→    r=64,
   478→    lora_alpha=128,            # alpha = 2 * rank is standard convention
   479→    target_modules=["q_proj", "v_proj"],  # attention projections only, no modules_to_save
   480→    lora_dropout=0.1,
   481→    bias="none",
   482→    task_type="CAUSAL_LM",    # required for decoder-only models (Qwen2.5, LLaMA, etc.)
   483→    # DO NOT add modules_to_save — breaks vLLM loading
   484→)
   485→```
   486→
   487→### Dataset.from_list with "messages" field
   488→```python
   489→# Source: TRL SFT docs dataset formats section
   490→# https://huggingface.co/docs/trl/en/sft_trainer#expected-dataset-type-and-format
   491→from datasets import Dataset
   492→
   493→# format_for_sft() returns: [{"role": "system", ...}, {"role": "user", ...}, {"role": "assistant", ...}]
   494→messages = format_for_sft(trajectory)  # list[dict]
   495→dataset = Dataset.from_list([{"messages": messages}])
   496→# SFTTrainer detects "messages" key → applies Qwen2.5's chat template automatically
   497→```
   498→
   499→### SFTTrainer with peft_config (saves adapter only)
   500→```python
   501→# Source: TRL docs PEFT integration section
   502→# https://huggingface.co/docs/trl/en/sft_trainer#train-adapters-with-peft
   503→from trl import SFTConfig, SFTTrainer
   504→
   505→training_args = SFTConfig(
   506→    output_dir=output_dir,
   507→    num_train_epochs=3,
   508→    learning_rate=2e-4,
   509→    warmup_ratio=0.03,
   510→    lr_scheduler_type="cosine",
   511→    bf16=True,
   512→    per_device_train_batch_size=1,
   513→    gradient_accumulation_steps=4,
   514→    save_strategy="no",   # disable intermediate checkpoints
   515→    logging_steps=1,
   516→    report_to="none",     # disable wandb/tensorboard in local training
   517→)
   518→trainer = SFTTrainer(
   519→    model=model,                    # pre-loaded quantized model OR model name string
   520→    args=training_args,
   521→    train_dataset=dataset,
   522→    peft_config=lora_config,        # triggers adapter-only training and saving
   523→    processing_class=tokenizer,
   524→)
   525→trainer.train()
   526→trainer.save_model(output_dir)      # saves adapter_model.safetensors + adapter_config.json ONLY
   527→```
   528→
   529→### apply_lora_adapter via get_peft_model
   530→```python
   531→# Source: https://huggingface.co/docs/peft/package_reference/lora#peft.LoraModel
   532→from peft import get_peft_model
   533→
   534→def apply_lora_adapter(model, config):
   535→    """Wrap base model with LoRA adapter for training."""
   536→    from peft import get_peft_model  # deferred import
   537→    return get_peft_model(model, config)
   538→```
   539→
   540→Note: When using SFTTrainer with peft_config, get_peft_model is called internally. apply_lora_adapter is more useful for inference-time adapter loading or when using the PEFT model outside SFTTrainer.
   541→
   542→### uv workspace dependency declaration (TRAIN-07)
   543→```toml
   544→# Source: existing workspace pattern (libs/adapter-registry in training-svc/pyproject.toml)
   545→# Add to services/training-svc/pyproject.toml:
   546→[project]
   547→dependencies = [
   548→    "adapter-registry",
   549→    "model-training",   # ADD THIS
   550→    "shared",
   551→    "fastapi>=0.110.0",
   552→    ...
   553→]
   554→
   555→[tool.uv.sources]
   556→adapter-registry = { workspace = true }
   557→model-training = { workspace = true }  # ADD THIS
   558→```
   559→
   560→### mypy overrides for GPU libraries (already exists in root pyproject.toml)
   561→```toml
   562→# Source: root pyproject.toml - already configured
   563→# peft, datasets, transformers, torch are already in [[tool.mypy.overrides]] with ignore_missing_imports = true
   564→# bitsandbytes and trl may need to be added if not already present
   565→[[tool.mypy.overrides]]
   566→module = [
   567→    "bitsandbytes",
   568→    "bitsandbytes.*",
   569→    "trl",
   570→    "trl.*",
   571→]
   572→ignore_missing_imports = true
   573→```
   574→
   575→---
   576→
   577→## State of the Art
   578→
   579→| Old Approach | Current Approach | When Changed | Impact |
   580→|--------------|------------------|--------------|--------|
   581→| SFTTrainer with dataset_text_field="text" | SFTTrainer with "messages" field (conversational format) | TRL v0.11+ | Chat template auto-applied; correct tokenization for Qwen2.5 |
   582→| Separate peft.prepare_model_for_kbit_training() call | SFTTrainer handles internally when peft_config given | TRL v0.7+ | One less manual step; reduces setup errors |
   583→| trainer.model.save_pretrained() | trainer.save_model() | TRL v0.8+ | Correctly routes to PEFT-aware saving when adapters are active |
   584→| LoraConfig without task_type | LoraConfig(task_type="CAUSAL_LM") | PEFT v0.4+ | task_type required for correct PEFT behavior with CausalLM |
   585→
   586→**Deprecated/outdated:**
   587→- `dataset_text_field` parameter: Still works for pre-formatted text strings, but the "messages" field approach is preferred for chat models — applies the model's actual chat template.
   588→- `transformers.Trainer` directly for QLoRA: SFTTrainer wraps Trainer and adds PEFT integration, completion-only loss, and chat template handling — use SFTTrainer.
   589→- `peft.prepare_model_for_kbit_training(model)` before SFTTrainer: SFTTrainer calls this internally when peft_config is provided. Calling it manually is now redundant (though harmless).
   590→
   591→---
   592→
   593→## Open Questions
   594→
   595→1. **Qwen2.5-Coder-7B base model string**
   596→   - What we know: CONTEXT.md specifies Qwen2.5-Coder-7B as the target model
   597→   - What's unclear: Should the default be "Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B" (base) or "Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct" (instruct-tuned)?
   598→   - Recommendation: Use env var RUNE_BASE_MODEL defaulting to "Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct" since SFT from instruction-tuned models typically performs better for code generation tasks; read inside function body for testability.
   599→
   600→2. **SFTConfig bf16 vs bfloat16 in training_args**
   601→   - What we know: SFTConfig inherits from TrainingArguments; bf16=True enables bfloat16 training
   602→   - What's unclear: Whether bf16=True interacts with BitsAndBytesConfig's bnb_4bit_compute_dtype=torch.bfloat16
   603→   - Recommendation: Set both — bf16=True in SFTConfig activates bfloat16 for the LoRA adapter computation; bnb_4bit_compute_dtype=torch.bfloat16 sets the dequantization compute dtype. They work at different levels and do not conflict.
   604→
   605→3. **SFTTrainer and single-example dataset**
   606→   - What we know: Single-trajectory training produces a dataset with exactly 1 example
   607→   - What's unclear: Whether SFTTrainer handles a 1-example dataset gracefully (no shuffle issues, no empty eval split)
   608→   - Recommendation: Pass `eval_dataset=None` explicitly (SFTTrainer default) and disable eval with `eval_strategy="no"` in SFTConfig. A 1-example dataset is valid for SFT.
   609→
   610→4. **Training output dir cleanup strategy**
   611→   - What we know: CONTEXT.md defers temp directory strategy to Claude's discretion
   612→   - Recommendation: Use `tempfile.mkdtemp()` as the SFTTrainer output_dir during training. After `trainer.save_model()`, copy adapter_model.safetensors and adapter_config.json to `~/.rune/adapters/{adapter_id}/` using `shutil.copy2()`. Then remove the temp dir with `shutil.rmtree()`. This avoids leaving checkpoint debris.
   613→
   614→5. **bitsandbytes and trl in root mypy overrides**
   615→   - What we know: Root pyproject.toml already has peft, datasets, transformers, torch in mypy overrides
   616→   - What's unclear: Whether bitsandbytes and trl are already listed
   617→   - Recommendation: Check root pyproject.toml and add `bitsandbytes`, `bitsandbytes.*`, `trl`, `trl.*` to the ignore_missing_imports override block if missing. Current root pyproject.toml only shows peft/torch/transformers/datasets — trl and bitsandbytes likely need to be added.
   618→
   619→---
   620→
   621→## Sources
   622→
   623→### Primary (HIGH confidence)
   624→- TRL official docs https://huggingface.co/docs/trl/en/sft_trainer - SFTTrainer API, dataset formats, peft_config parameter, save_model behavior
   625→- PEFT official docs https://huggingface.co/docs/peft/package_reference/lora - LoraConfig parameters, task_type, modules_to_save behavior
   626→- FastAPI official docs https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/background-tasks/ - BackgroundTasks API, in-memory tracking pattern
   627→- Project codebase (Phase 18-20) - established patterns for deferred imports, session-per-method, workspace dependencies, env var reads inside function bodies
   628→
   629→### Secondary (MEDIUM confidence)
   630→- vLLM GitHub issue #9280 https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/issues/9280 - confirmed modules_to_save breaks vLLM LoRA loading
   631→- HuggingFace transformers quantization docs https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/en/quantization/bitsandbytes - BitsAndBytesConfig parameters
   632→- kaitchup.substack.com "Qwen2.5 QLoRA, LoRA, and Full Fine-tuning" - Qwen2.5 target_modules verification
   633→
   634→### Tertiary (LOW confidence)
   635→- WebSearch results on SFTTrainer single-example dataset behavior - needs empirical verification during implementation
   636→
   637→---
   638→
   639→## Metadata
   640→
   641→**Confidence breakdown:**
   642→- Standard stack: HIGH - verified via official HuggingFace docs (TRL, PEFT, transformers)
   643→- Architecture: HIGH - SFTTrainer + peft_config pattern is the documented standard approach; FastAPI BackgroundTasks pattern is established
   644→- Pitfalls: HIGH - modules_to_save/vLLM incompatibility verified via vLLM GitHub issue; import deferral pattern established in Phase 19-20
   645→- Schema change (session_id): HIGH - LoraTrainingRequest clearly missing session_id field based on codebase inspection
   646→
   647→**Research date:** 2026-03-05
   648→**Valid until:** 2026-06-05 (stable libraries; TRL/PEFT APIs change slowly)
   649→

1→"""Trajectory recording and formatting for coding session distillation.
     2→
     3→Provides functions to persist, load, and convert coding session trajectories
     4→into SFT-compatible chat format for LoRA fine-tuning pipelines.
     5→"""
     6→
     7→from __future__ import annotations
     8→
     9→import json
    10→import os
    11→from datetime import datetime, timezone
    12→from pathlib import Path
    13→from typing import Any, Optional
    14→
    15→SYSTEM_PROMPT = "You are a Python code generator. Output only code, no explanation."
    16→
    17→
    18→def _get_trajectory_dir() -> Path:
    19→    """Return the trajectory storage directory, respecting RUNE_TRAJECTORY_DIR env var.
    20→
    21→    Reads env var inside function body (not module level) so that monkeypatch.setenv()
    22→    works correctly in tests.
    23→    """
    24→    env_dir = os.environ.get("RUNE_TRAJECTORY_DIR")
    25→    if env_dir:
    26→        return Path(env_dir)
    27→    return Path.home() / ".rune" / "trajectories"
    28→
    29→
    30→def record_trajectory(
    31→    session_id: str,
    32→    steps: list[dict[str, Any]],
    33→    outcome: Optional[str] = None,
    34→    *,
    35→    task_description: str = "",
    36→    task_type: str = "",
    37→    adapter_ids: list[str] | None = None,
    38→) -> dict[str, Any]:
    39→    """Persist a coding session trajectory to disk for future distillation.
    40→
    41→    Args:
    42→        session_id: Unique identifier for the coding session.
    43→        steps: List of step dicts, each containing attempt results.
    44→        outcome: Final session result ('success', 'exhausted', or None).
    45→        task_description: Natural language description of the coding task.
    46→        task_type: Category of task (e.g. 'function', 'class', 'refactor').
    47→        adapter_ids: LoRA adapter IDs used during the session.
    48→
    49→    Returns:
    50→        A dict with 'session_id' and 'file_path' keys.
    51→    """
    52→    trajectory_dir = _get_trajectory_dir()
    53→    trajectory_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    54→
    55→    file_path = trajectory_dir / f"{session_id}.json"
    56→    timestamp = datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc).isoformat()
    57→
    58→    trajectory: dict[str, Any] = {
    59→        "session_id": session_id,
    60→        "task_description": task_description,
    61→        "task_type": task_type,
    62→        "adapter_ids": adapter_ids if adapter_ids is not None else [],
    63→        "outcome": outcome,
    64→        "timestamp": timestamp,
    65→        "steps": steps,
    66→    }
    67→
    68→    file_path.write_text(json.dumps(trajectory, indent=2))
    69→
    70→    return {"session_id": session_id, "file_path": str(file_path)}
    71→
    72→
    73→def load_trajectory(trajectory_id: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
    74→    """Load a stored trajectory by session ID.
    75→
    76→    Args:
    77→        trajectory_id: The session ID used as the filename (without .json).
    78→
    79→    Returns:
    80→        A dict containing the full trajectory data including steps and metadata.
    81→
    82→    Raises:
    83→        FileNotFoundError: If no trajectory file exists for the given ID.
    84→    """
    85→    trajectory_dir = _get_trajectory_dir()
    86→    file_path = trajectory_dir / f"{trajectory_id}.json"
    87→    # Let FileNotFoundError propagate naturally if file does not exist
    88→    return json.loads(file_path.read_text())  # type: ignore[no-any-return]
    89→
    90→
    91→def format_for_sft(trajectory: dict[str, Any]) -> list[dict[str, str]]:
    92→    """Convert a trajectory into SFT-compatible chat format.
    93→
    94→    Only successful trajectories (outcome == 'success') produce output.
    95→    Extracts the final step where tests_passed is True as the assistant message.
    96→
    97→    Args:
    98→        trajectory: A trajectory dict as returned by load_trajectory.
    99→
   100→    Returns:
   101→        A list of 3 message dicts ([system, user, assistant]) for successful
   102→        trajectories, or an empty list if the trajectory did not succeed.
   103→    """
   104→    if trajectory.get("outcome") != "success":
   105→        return []
   106→
   107→    steps: list[dict[str, Any]] = trajectory.get("steps", [])
   108→    successful_step = next(
   109→        (s for s in reversed(steps) if s.get("tests_passed")),
   110→        None,
   111→    )
   112→
   113→    if successful_step is None:
   114→        return []
   115→
   116→    task_description: str = trajectory.get("task_description", "")
   117→    generated_code: str = successful_step.get("generated_code", "")
   118→
   119→    return [
   120→        {"role": "system", "content": SYSTEM_PROMPT},
   121→        {"role": "user", "content": task_description},
   122→        {"role": "assistant", "content": generated_code},
   123→    ]
   124→

1→"""Training pipelines for LoRA fine-tuning and trajectory management."""
     2→
     3→from model_training.trajectory import format_for_sft, load_trajectory, record_trajectory
     4→

1→"""SQLModel table model for LoRA adapter metadata records."""
     2→
     3→from typing import Optional
     4→
     5→from sqlmodel import Field, SQLModel
     6→
     7→
     8→class AdapterRecord(SQLModel, table=True):
     9→    """A stored LoRA adapter metadata record.
    10→
    11→    Tracks metadata for a single LoRA adapter including its lineage,
    12→    storage location, and evaluation metrics. Backed by SQLite via SQLModel.
    13→
    14→    Attributes:
    15→        id: Unique adapter identifier (UUID string).
    16→        version: Adapter version number for lineage tracking.
    17→        task_type: Task category this adapter was trained on (e.g. 'bug-fix').
    18→        base_model_id: Identifier of the base model this adapter was trained from.
    19→        rank: LoRA rank used during training.
    20→        created_at: ISO 8601 timestamp of adapter creation.
    21→        file_path: Filesystem path to the adapter weights file.
    22→        file_hash: SHA-256 hash of the adapter weights file for integrity checks.
    23→        file_size_bytes: Size of the adapter weights file in bytes.
    24→        pass_rate: Pass rate on benchmark tasks (0.0 to 1.0), if evaluated.
    25→        fitness_score: Overall evolutionary fitness score, if evaluated.
    26→        source: How the adapter was created ('distillation', 'evolution', 'manual').
    27→        session_id: ID of the coding session that produced this adapter.
    28→        is_archived: Whether this adapter has been archived (soft delete).
    29→
    30→    Example:
    31→        >>> record = AdapterRecord(
    32→        ...     id="adapter-001", version=1, task_type="bug-fix",
    33→        ...     base_model_id="Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B", rank=16,
    34→        ...     created_at="2026-01-01T00:00:00Z",
    35→        ...     file_path="/adapters/adapter-001.safetensors",
    36→        ...     file_hash="abc123", file_size_bytes=1024,
    37→        ...     source="distillation", session_id="sess-001",
    38→        ... )
    39→        >>> record.task_type
    40→        'bug-fix'
    41→        >>> record.is_archived
    42→        False
    43→    """
    44→
    45→    __tablename__ = "adapter_records"
    46→
    47→    id: str = Field(primary_key=True)
    48→    version: int
    49→    task_type: str = Field(index=True)
    50→    base_model_id: str
    51→    rank: int
    52→    created_at: str
    53→    file_path: str
    54→    file_hash: str
    55→    file_size_bytes: int
    56→    pass_rate: Optional[float] = Field(default=None)
    57→    fitness_score: Optional[float] = Field(default=None)
    58→    source: str
    59→    session_id: str
    60→    is_archived: bool = Field(default=False)
    61→

  <action>
**trainer.py** (NEW file) — Create the `train_qlora()` orchestrator function. ALL GPU imports MUST be deferred inside the function body per INFRA-05. This function orchestrates the entire training pipeline.

```python
def train_qlora(
    session_id: str,
    adapter_id: str,
    output_dir: str,
    *,
    base_model_id: str | None = None,
    task_type: str = "code-gen",
    rank: int = 64,
    alpha: int = 128,
    epochs: int = 3,
    learning_rate: float = 2e-4,
) -> str:
```

Implementation steps inside the function body:
1. Deferred imports: `from datasets import Dataset`, `from transformers import AutoModelForCausalLM, AutoTokenizer, BitsAndBytesConfig`, `from trl import SFTConfig, SFTTrainer`, `import torch`
2. Import from model_training: `from model_training.peft_utils import build_qlora_config`, `from model_training.trajectory import load_trajectory, format_for_sft`
3. Read RUNE_BASE_MODEL env var inside function body (default "Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct") if base_model_id is None. Use `os.environ.get("RUNE_BASE_MODEL", "Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct")`.
4. Load trajectory via `load_trajectory(session_id)`, format via `format_for_sft(trajectory)`. If messages is empty, raise `ValueError(f"Trajectory {session_id} is not successful or has no SFT messages")`.
5. Create dataset: `Dataset.from_list([{"messages": messages}])`
6. Build BitsAndBytesConfig: load_in_4bit=True, bnb_4bit_quant_type="nf4", bnb_4bit_compute_dtype=torch.bfloat16, bnb_4bit_use_double_quant=True
7. Load model: `AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(model_id, quantization_config=bnb_config, device_map="auto")`
8. Load tokenizer: `AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(model_id)`
9. Build LoRA config: `build_qlora_config(rank=rank, alpha=alpha, target_modules=["q_proj", "v_proj"], dropout=0.1)`
10. Create SFTConfig with: output_dir, num_train_epochs=epochs, learning_rate=learning_rate, warmup_ratio=0.03, lr_scheduler_type="cosine", bf16=True, per_device_train_batch_size=1, gradient_accumulation_steps=4, save_strategy="no", logging_steps=1, report_to="none", eval_strategy="no"
11. Create SFTTrainer: model=model, args=training_args, train_dataset=dataset, peft_config=lora_config, processing_class=tokenizer
12. Call trainer.train()
13. Call trainer.save_model(output_dir) — saves adapter_model.safetensors + adapter_config.json only (PEFT-aware save)
14. Return output_dir

Add a second helper function for the full pipeline including registry storage:

```python
def train_and_register(
    session_id: str,
    adapter_id: str,
    *,
    base_model_id: str | None = None,
    task_type: str = "code-gen",
    rank: int = 64,
    alpha: int = 128,
    epochs: int = 3,
    learning_rate: float = 2e-4,
    database_url: str | None = None,
) -> str:
```

This function:
1. Creates adapter output dir: `~/.rune/adapters/{adapter_id}/` (create parents, exist_ok=True). Read RUNE_ADAPTER_DIR env var inside function body, default to `Path.home() / ".rune" / "adapters"`.
2. Calls train_qlora() with the adapter dir as output_dir
3. Builds an AdapterRecord:
   - id=adapter_id, version=1, task_type=task_type, base_model_id (from env or param), rank=rank
   - created_at=datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc).isoformat()
   - file_path=str(adapter_dir), file_hash=sha256 of adapter_model.safetensors, file_size_bytes from stat
   - source="qlora", session_id=session_id
4. Creates AdapterRegistry with engine from database_url (or RUNE_DATABASE_URL env var, default "sqlite:///~/.rune/rune.db")
5. Calls registry.store(record)
6. Returns adapter_id

Use `from __future__ import annotations` at top. Module-level imports: only stdlib (os, hashlib, pathlib, datetime). Everything else deferred inside function bodies.

**__init__.py** — Add exports for new public functions. Do NOT import from peft_utils or trainer at the module level (this would trigger GPU imports). Instead, add the names to __all__ and import them lazily or just document that users import from the submodules directly.

```python
"""In-memory training job status tracking.

Module-level JOB_STORE dict shared across all request handlers.
State is lost on service restart — acceptable for single-user local MVP.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Optional

JOB_STORE: dict[str, JobStatus] = {}


@dataclass
class JobStatus:
    """Training job status tracker."""
    job_id: str
    status: str  # "queued" | "running" | "completed" | "failed"
    adapter_id: Optional[str] = None
    error: Optional[str] = None
```

POST /train/lora implementation:
1. Import `uuid` at top (stdlib). Import `BackgroundTasks` from fastapi.
2. Import `from training_svc.jobs import JOB_STORE, JobStatus` at top (no GPU deps).
3. Generate job_id and adapter_id (or use request.adapter_id if provided) via `str(uuid.uuid4())`.
4. Create JOB_STORE entry BEFORE adding background task: `JOB_STORE[job_id] = JobStatus(job_id=job_id, status="queued", adapter_id=adapter_id)`.
5. Add background task `_run_training_job` with params: job_id, session_id, adapter_id, task_type, rank, epochs, learning_rate.
6. Return `JSONResponse(content={"job_id": job_id, "status": "queued"}, status_code=200)`.
7. Function signature: `async def train_lora(request: LoraTrainingRequest, background_tasks: BackgroundTasks) -> JSONResponse:`

`_run_training_job` helper (module-level function, not async):
1. Update JOB_STORE[job_id].status = "running"
2. Try: import `from model_training.trainer import train_and_register` INSIDE the function body (deferred GPU import per INFRA-05)
3. Call train_and_register(session_id=session_id, adapter_id=adapter_id, task_type=task_type, rank=rank, epochs=epochs, learning_rate=learning_rate)
4. On success: JOB_STORE[job_id].status = "completed"
5. On exception: JOB_STORE[job_id].status = "failed", JOB_STORE[job_id].error = str(e)

GET /jobs/{job_id} implementation:
1. Look up job in JOB_STORE. If not found, raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f"Job {job_id} not found").
2. Return JSONResponse with job.job_id, job.status, job.adapter_id, job.error.

Import HTTPException from fastapi at top.

- [x] **Phase 18: Adapter Registry** — SQLite-backed CRUD hub that all other components read from and write to (2/2 plans complete)
- [x] **Phase 19: Inference Provider Abstraction** — Abstract InferenceProvider interface with vLLM and Ollama implementations, provider factory, and lora-server configuration (3/3 plans complete)
- [x] **Phase 20: Agent Loop** — Backend-agnostic generate → execute → reflect → save_trajectory cycle with trajectory persistence (2/2 plans complete)
- [ ] **Phase 21: QLoRA Training Pipeline** — Full gradient-descent training path from trajectory to PEFT adapter stored in registry, with training-svc HTTP dispatch
- [ ] **Phase 22: Kill-Switch Gate** — Doc-to-LoRA hypernetwork + evaluation lib measuring the 5% Pass@1 improvement threshold

## Phase Details

### Phase 18: Adapter Registry
**Goal**: Users can persist, retrieve, and query LoRA adapter metadata via a SQLite-backed registry that all other components share
**Depends on**: Nothing (first v5.0 phase)
**Requirements**: AREG-01, AREG-02, AREG-03, AREG-04, AREG-05
**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
  1. User can call AdapterRegistry.store() with full metadata and retrieve the same record by ID with no data loss
  2. User can call AdapterRegistry.query_by_task_type() and receive only adapters matching the specified task type
  3. User can call AdapterRegistry.list_all() and receive all non-archived records (archived records excluded)
  4. Multiple FastAPI requests can read and write concurrently without deadlock (WAL mode confirmed via PRAGMA)
  5. AdapterRegistry constructor accepts an explicit engine parameter so all services share one SQLite file via DATABASE_URL
**Plans**: 2 plans

Plans:
- [x] 18-01-PLAN.md — Implement AdapterRegistry: constructor (Engine + WAL hook + create_all) and 4 CRUD methods — DONE 2026-03-05
- [x] 18-02-PLAN.md — Pivot tests from red to green: update conftest fixtures, replace NotImplementedError assertions, add WAL and concurrency integration tests — DONE 2026-03-05

### Phase 19: Inference Provider Abstraction
**Goal**: Users can generate completions and hot-load/unload LoRA adapters through a provider-agnostic interface, with vLLM and Ollama backends selectable by configuration and a correctly configured lora-server
**Depends on**: Phase 18
**Requirements**: INF-01, INF-02, INF-03, INF-04, INF-05, INF-06, INF-07, INFRA-01, INFRA-02, INFRA-03
**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
  1. InferenceProvider interface exists with generate(), load_adapter(), unload_adapter(), and list_adapters() methods that both backends implement
  2. Provider factory returns a VLLMProvider or OllamaProvider instance based on an environment variable or config file with no code changes required
  3. User can call provider.generate() with an adapter name and receive adapter-conditioned output from the active backend
  4. User can load two adapters simultaneously via VLLMProvider and generate from each by name without collision
  5. Agent can configure different models or providers per step (e.g., VLLMProvider for generation, OllamaProvider for reflection) without changing agent graph structure
  6. lora-server starts without error with VLLM_ALLOW_RUNTIME_LORA_UPDATING=True set, and docker-compose runs all services without port conflict
**Plans**: 3 plans

Plans:
- [x] 19-01-PLAN.md — InferenceProvider ABC + GenerationResult + VLLMProvider + OllamaProvider with full test coverage — DONE 2026-03-05
- [x] 19-02-PLAN.md — lora-server Dockerfile update (vLLM base image + runtime LoRA env) and docker-compose port conflict fix — DONE 2026-03-05
- [x] 19-03-PLAN.md — Provider factory with instance cache + per-step config + __init__.py exports + delete old stubs — DONE 2026-03-05

### Phase 20: Agent Loop
**Goal**: Users can invoke the Rune agent on a coding task and observe a complete generate → execute → reflect → save_trajectory cycle through the InferenceProvider interface, with trajectory data persisted to disk
**Depends on**: Phase 18, Phase 19
**Requirements**: AGENT-01, AGENT-02, AGENT-03, AGENT-04, AGENT-05, AGENT-06, TRAIN-01, TRAIN-02
**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
  1. Agent invocation on a coding task produces generated code from generate_node via InferenceProvider.generate() (backend-agnostic — same call path for vLLM or Ollama)
  2. execute_node runs the generated code in a subprocess sandbox and returns stdout, stderr, exit_code, and tests_passed within the timeout
  3. reflect_node accumulates attempt count and trajectory data without making any LLM call
  4. save_trajectory_node writes a structured JSON trajectory file (session_id, steps, outcome) readable after the session ends
  5. Agent loop closes end-to-end: retries on failure up to max_attempts, then terminates with either success or exhausted outcome; RuneState includes session_id field
**Plans**: 2 plans

Plans:
- [x] 20-01-PLAN.md — Trajectory library (record, load, format_for_sft) + RuneState session_id + model-training workspace dep — DONE 2026-03-05
- [x] 20-02-PLAN.md — All 4 node implementations (generate, execute, reflect, save_trajectory) + green-phase test rewrites — DONE 2026-03-05

### Phase 21: QLoRA Training Pipeline
**Goal**: Users can trigger end-to-end QLoRA training from a recorded trajectory, producing a PEFT safetensors adapter stored in the registry and accessible for loading via the inference provider
**Depends on**: Phase 18, Phase 20
**Requirements**: TRAIN-03, TRAIN-04, TRAIN-05, TRAIN-06, TRAIN-07, INFRA-04, INFRA-05
**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
  1. User can call build_qlora_config() and receive a PEFT LoraConfig with NF4 quantization and bfloat16 compute dtype
  2. User can call apply_lora_adapter() to attach a LoRA adapter to a base model for inference or further training
  3. POST /train/lora with a session_id runs the full trajectory → SFT format → PEFT train → save safetensors → registry.store() pipeline and returns a job_id
  4. Trained adapter files are in standard PEFT safetensors format (no embed_tokens keys) compatible with vLLM dynamic loading via InferenceProvider.load_adapter()
  5. GPU imports (peft, bitsandbytes, transformers, trl, datasets) are deferred inside function bodies and do not cause ImportError in CPU-only CI
**Plans**: 2 plans

Plans:
- [ ] 21-01-PLAN.md — Implement model-training library: peft_utils, config, train_qlora orchestrator, GPU deps, mypy overrides
- [ ] 21-02-PLAN.md — Wire training-svc endpoints: POST /train/lora + GET /jobs/{job_id} with background job dispatch

### Phase 22: Kill-Switch Gate
**Goal**: Users can measure whether Doc-to-LoRA-generated adapters improve Pass@1 on a HumanEval subset by at least 5% relative to the base model, validating the core hypothesis
**Depends on**: Phase 20, Phase 21
**Requirements**: DTOL-01, DTOL-02, DTOL-03, DTOL-04, EVAL-01, EVAL-02, EVAL-03
**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
  1. DocToLoraHypernetwork exists in model-training lib, accepts a coding trajectory, and returns rank-8 LoRA adapter weights in a single forward pass under 1 second
  2. Generated adapter weights are in standard PEFT safetensors format and can be loaded into vLLM via InferenceProvider.load_adapter() without error
  3. User can call run_humaneval_subset() and receive pass/fail results for each task in the 20-task subset
  4. User can call calculate_pass_at_k() and receive a Pass@1 score for both baseline and adapter-enhanced configurations
  5. Kill-switch gate comparison prints a clear verdict: PASS (>=5% improvement) or FAIL (hypothesis rejected), with both baseline and adapter Pass@1 scores shown
**Plans**: TBD

## Progress

content.text:
## PLANNING COMPLETE

**Phase:** 21-qlora-training-pipeline
**Plans:** 2 plans in 2 waves

### Wave Structure

| Wave | Plans | Autonomous |
|------|-------|------------|
| 1 | 21-01 | yes |
| 2 | 21-02 | yes |

### Plans Created

| Plan | Objective | Tasks | Requirements | Files |
|------|-----------|-------|--------------|-------|
| 21-01 | Model-training library: peft_utils, config, train_qlora orchestrator, GPU deps, mypy overrides | 2 | TRAIN-03, TRAIN-04, TRAIN-05, INFRA-04, INFRA-05 | peft_utils.py, config.py, trainer.py, __init__.py, pyproject.toml (x2), 3 test files |
| 21-02 | Training-svc endpoints: POST /train/lora + GET /jobs/{job_id} with background job dispatch | 2 | TRAIN-06, TRAIN-07 | pyproject.toml, schemas.py, jobs.py, training.py, test_training.py |

### Requirement Coverage

| Requirement | Plan | Task |
|-------------|------|------|
| TRAIN-03 (build_qlora_config) | 21-01 | Task 1 |
| TRAIN-04 (apply_lora_adapter) | 21-01 | Task 1 |
| TRAIN-05 (end-to-end pipeline) | 21-01 | Task 2 |
| TRAIN-06 (POST /train/lora) | 21-02 | Task 2 |
| TRAIN-07 (workspace dependency) | 21-02 | Task 1 |
| INFRA-04 (GPU deps in pyproject) | 21-01 | Task 1 |
| INFRA-05 (deferred GPU imports) | 21-01 | Tasks 1+2 |

### Key Design Decisions

- **Vertical slice**: Plan 01 is the library layer (model-training), Plan 02 is the service layer (training-svc). Plan 02 depends on Plan 01 because the endpoint calls `train_and_register()`.
- **train_qlora() orchestrator** in new `trainer.py` module keeps the endpoint handler thin and enables isolated testing of the training pipeline logic.
- **train_and_register()** wraps train_qlora + AdapterRegistry.store() for full pipeline in one call.
- All GPU imports deferred per established Phase 19-20 pattern. Tests use mocking and xfail for GPU-dependent assertions.
- Root pyproject.toml mypy overrides need `bitsandbytes` and `trl` added (currently missing).

### Next Steps

Execute: `/gsd:execute-phase 21-qlora-training-pipeline`

`/clear` first - fresh context window

content.content:
1→# Phase 21: QLoRA Training Pipeline - Context
     2→
     3→**Gathered:** 2026-03-05
     4→**Status:** Ready for planning
     5→
     6→<domain>
     7→## Phase Boundary
     8→
     9→End-to-end QLoRA training pipeline: trajectory data → SFT dataset → PEFT QLoRA training → safetensors output → adapter registry storage. Also wires training-svc POST /train/lora endpoint with async background job tracking, and adds GPU dependencies to model-training pyproject.toml with deferred imports.
    10→
    11→</domain>
    12→
    13→<decisions>
    14→## Implementation Decisions
    15→
    16→### Training data flow
    17→- Single-trajectory training per run — one session's successful trajectory produces one adapter
    18→- `format_for_sft()` output (list[dict]) wrapped into a HuggingFace `Dataset` via `Dataset.from_list()`
    19→- SFT format: system/user/assistant chat turns → TRL's SFTTrainer handles tokenization
    20→- No batching across trajectories for v5.0 — each training run uses one trajectory
    21→
    22→### QLoRA configuration
    23→- bfloat16 compute dtype hardcoded (carried from project decisions — float16 causes NaN at 7B)
    24→- NF4 quantization via BitsAndBytesConfig (4-bit quantization type)
    25→- Default rank=64, alpha=128 (2x rank), target_modules=["q_proj", "v_proj"]
    26→- Hyperparameters: lr=2e-4, epochs=3, warmup_ratio=0.03, cosine scheduler
    27→- `build_qlora_config()` returns peft LoraConfig, `get_training_config()` returns training hyperparams dict
    28→
    29→### Training service design
    30→- FastAPI BackgroundTasks for async execution — no external task queue
    31→- In-memory `dict[str, JobStatus]` for job tracking (dataclass with job_id, status, adapter_id, error)
    32→- POST /train/lora: accepts trajectory session_id + optional overrides → returns job_id immediately
    33→- GET /jobs/{job_id}: returns current status (queued/running/completed/failed + adapter_id when done)
    34→- training-svc adds model-training as workspace dependency (TRAIN-07)
    35→- Loses state on restart — acceptable for single-user local MVP
    36→
    37→### Adapter output & registration
    38→- Trained adapters saved to `~/.rune/adapters/{adapter_id}/` as safetensors
    39→- adapter_id generated as UUID at training start
    40→- Auto-register in AdapterRegistry.store() after successful training with full metadata (task_type, base_model_id, rank, file_path, file_hash, source="qlora")
    41→- Save adapter_config.json alongside safetensors for PEFT compatibility / vLLM loading
    42→
    43→### GPU import strategy (INFRA-04/05)
    44→- model-training pyproject.toml adds: peft, bitsandbytes, transformers, trl, datasets
    45→- All GPU imports (peft, bitsandbytes, transformers, trl) deferred inside function bodies
    46→- TYPE_CHECKING guards for type annotations only
    47→- CPU-only CI must pass: import model_training succeeds without GPU libs installed
    48→
    49→### Claude's Discretion
    50→- Exact SFTTrainer configuration parameters beyond those specified
    51→- Error handling and cleanup on training failure
    52→- Temp directory strategy during training
    53→- Whether to add a `train_qlora()` orchestrator function or keep steps separate
    54→
    55→</decisions>
    56→
    57→<specifics>
    58→## Specific Ideas
    59→
    60→- Training pipeline follows the chain: load trajectory → format_for_sft() → Dataset.from_list() → SFTTrainer → save_pretrained() → store in registry
    61→- PEFT save_pretrained() outputs adapter_model.safetensors + adapter_config.json — this is the standard format vLLM expects for runtime LoRA loading
    62→- Base model for v5.0: Qwen2.5-Coder-7B (matches hardware target of 24GB per GPU with QLoRA)
    63→
    64→</specifics>

    55→### v5.0 First Implementation (In Progress)
    56→
    57→**Milestone Goal:** Transform wireframe stubs into a working end-to-end system — adapter registry to provider-agnostic inference to agent loop to QLoRA training to Doc-to-LoRA kill-switch gate — running entirely on local hardware.
    58→

    60→- [x] **Phase 19: Inference Provider Abstraction** — Abstract InferenceProvider interface with vLLM and Ollama implementations, provider factory, and lora-server configuration (3/3 plans complete)
    61→- [x] **Phase 20: Agent Loop** — Backend-agnostic generate → execute → reflect → save_trajectory cycle with trajectory persistence (2/2 plans complete)
    62→- [ ] **Phase 21: QLoRA Training Pipeline** — Full gradient-descent training path from trajectory to PEFT adapter stored in registry, with training-svc HTTP dispatch

    64→
    65→## Phase Details
    66→
    67→### Phase 18: Adapter Registry
    68→**Goal**: Users can persist, retrieve, and query LoRA adapter metadata via a SQLite-backed registry that all other components share
    69→**Depends on**: Nothing (first v5.0 phase)
    70→**Requirements**: AREG-01, AREG-02, AREG-03, AREG-04, AREG-05
    71→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
    72→  1. User can call AdapterRegistry.store() with full metadata and retrieve the same record by ID with no data loss
    73→  2. User can call AdapterRegistry.query_by_task_type() and receive only adapters matching the specified task type
    74→  3. User can call AdapterRegistry.list_all() and receive all non-archived records (archived records excluded)
    75→  4. Multiple FastAPI requests can read and write concurrently without deadlock (WAL mode confirmed via PRAGMA)
    76→  5. AdapterRegistry constructor accepts an explicit engine parameter so all services share one SQLite file via DATABASE_URL
    77→**Plans**: 2 plans
    78→
    79→Plans:
    80→- [x] 18-01-PLAN.md — Implement AdapterRegistry: constructor (Engine + WAL hook + create_all) and 4 CRUD methods — DONE 2026-03-05
    81→- [x] 18-02-PLAN.md — Pivot tests from red to green: update conftest fixtures, replace NotImplementedError assertions, add WAL and concurrency integration tests — DONE 2026-03-05
    82→
    83→### Phase 19: Inference Provider Abstraction
    84→**Goal**: Users can generate completions and hot-load/unload LoRA adapters through a provider-agnostic interface, with vLLM and Ollama backends selectable by configuration and a correctly configured lora-server
    85→**Depends on**: Phase 18
    86→**Requirements**: INF-01, INF-02, INF-03, INF-04, INF-05, INF-06, INF-07, INFRA-01, INFRA-02, INFRA-03
    87→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
    88→  1. InferenceProvider interface exists with generate(), load_adapter(), unload_adapter(), and list_adapters() methods that both backends implement
    89→  2. Provider factory returns a VLLMProvider or OllamaProvider instance based on an environment variable or config file with no code changes required
    90→  3. User can call provider.generate() with an adapter name and receive adapter-conditioned output from the active backend
    91→  4. User can load two adapters simultaneously via VLLMProvider and generate from each by name without collision
    92→  5. Agent can configure different models or providers per step (e.g., VLLMProvider for generation, OllamaProvider for reflection) without changing agent graph structure
    93→  6. lora-server starts without error with VLLM_ALLOW_RUNTIME_LORA_UPDATING=True set, and docker-compose runs all services without port conflict
    94→**Plans**: 3 plans
    95→
    96→Plans:
    97→- [x] 19-01-PLAN.md — InferenceProvider ABC + GenerationResult + VLLMProvider + OllamaProvider with full test coverage — DONE 2026-03-05
    98→- [x] 19-02-PLAN.md — lora-server Dockerfile update (vLLM base image + runtime LoRA env) and docker-compose port conflict fix — DONE 2026-03-05
    99→- [x] 19-03-PLAN.md — Provider factory with instance cache + per-step config + __init__.py exports + delete old stubs — DONE 2026-03-05
   100→
   101→### Phase 20: Agent Loop
   102→**Goal**: Users can invoke the Rune agent on a coding task and observe a complete generate → execute → reflect → save_trajectory cycle through the InferenceProvider interface, with trajectory data persisted to disk
   103→**Depends on**: Phase 18, Phase 19
   104→**Requirements**: AGENT-01, AGENT-02, AGENT-03, AGENT-04, AGENT-05, AGENT-06, TRAIN-01, TRAIN-02
   105→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   106→  1. Agent invocation on a coding task produces generated code from generate_node via InferenceProvider.generate() (backend-agnostic — same call path for vLLM or Ollama)
   107→  2. execute_node runs the generated code in a subprocess sandbox and returns stdout, stderr, exit_code, and tests_passed within the timeout
   108→  3. reflect_node accumulates attempt count and trajectory data without making any LLM call
   109→  4. save_trajectory_node writes a structured JSON trajectory file (session_id, steps, outcome) readable after the session ends
   110→  5. Agent loop closes end-to-end: retries on failure up to max_attempts, then terminates with either success or exhausted outcome; RuneState includes session_id field
   111→**Plans**: 2 plans
   112→
   113→Plans:
   114→- [x] 20-01-PLAN.md — Trajectory library (record, load, format_for_sft) + RuneState session_id + model-training workspace dep — DONE 2026-03-05
   115→- [x] 20-02-PLAN.md — All 4 node implementations (generate, execute, reflect, save_trajectory) + green-phase test rewrites — DONE 2026-03-05
   116→
   117→### Phase 21: QLoRA Training Pipeline
   118→**Goal**: Users can trigger end-to-end QLoRA training from a recorded trajectory, producing a PEFT safetensors adapter stored in the registry and accessible for loading via the inference provider
   119→**Depends on**: Phase 18, Phase 20
   120→**Requirements**: TRAIN-03, TRAIN-04, TRAIN-05, TRAIN-06, TRAIN-07, INFRA-04, INFRA-05
   121→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   122→  1. User can call build_qlora_config() and receive a PEFT LoraConfig with NF4 quantization and bfloat16 compute dtype
   123→  2. User can call apply_lora_adapter() to attach a LoRA adapter to a base model for inference or further training
   124→  3. POST /train/lora with a session_id runs the full trajectory → SFT format → PEFT train → save safetensors → registry.store() pipeline and returns a job_id
   125→  4. Trained adapter files are in standard PEFT safetensors format (no embed_tokens keys) compatible with vLLM dynamic loading via InferenceProvider.load_adapter()
   126→  5. GPU imports (peft, bitsandbytes, transformers, trl, datasets) are deferred inside function bodies and do not cause ImportError in CPU-only CI
   127→**Plans**: 2 plans
   128→
   129→Plans:
   130→- [ ] 21-01-PLAN.md — Implement model-training library: peft_utils, config, train_qlora orchestrator, GPU deps, mypy overrides
   131→- [ ] 21-02-PLAN.md — Wire training-svc endpoints: POST /train/lora + GET /jobs/{job_id} with background job dispatch
   132→
   133→### Phase 22: Kill-Switch Gate
   134→**Goal**: Users can measure whether Doc-to-LoRA-generated adapters improve Pass@1 on a HumanEval subset by at least 5% relative to the base model, validating the core hypothesis
   135→**Depends on**: Phase 20, Phase 21
   136→**Requirements**: DTOL-01, DTOL-02, DTOL-03, DTOL-04, EVAL-01, EVAL-02, EVAL-03
   137→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   138→  1. DocToLoraHypernetwork exists in model-training lib, accepts a coding trajectory, and returns rank-8 LoRA adapter weights in a single forward pass under 1 second
   139→  2. Generated adapter weights are in standard PEFT safetensors format and can be loaded into vLLM via InferenceProvider.load_adapter() without error
   140→  3. User can call run_humaneval_subset() and receive pass/fail results for each task in the 20-task subset
   141→  4. User can call calculate_pass_at_k() and receive a Pass@1 score for both baseline and adapter-enhanced configurations
   142→  5. Kill-switch gate comparison prints a clear verdict: PASS (>=5% improvement) or FAIL (hypothesis rejected), with both baseline and adapter Pass@1 scores shown
   143→**Plans**: TBD

1→# Requirements: Rune
     2→
     3→**Defined:** 2026-03-05
     4→**Core Value:** A local coding agent that learns from its own coding trajectories, building persistent parametric memory that scales independently of context window size.
     5→
     6→## v5.0 Requirements
     7→
     8→Requirements for first working implementation. Each maps to roadmap phases.
     9→
    10→### Adapter Registry
    11→
    12→- [x] **AREG-01**: User can store a new adapter record with metadata (task_type, base_model_id, rank, file_path, file_hash, source) via AdapterRegistry.store()
    13→- [x] **AREG-02**: User can retrieve an adapter record by ID via AdapterRegistry.retrieve_by_id()
    14→- [x] **AREG-03**: User can query adapters by task_type via AdapterRegistry.query_by_task_type()
    15→- [x] **AREG-04**: User can list all non-archived adapters via AdapterRegistry.list_all()
    16→- [x] **AREG-05**: AdapterRegistry uses SQLite with WAL mode and engine-parameterized constructor for shared database access
    17→
    18→### Inference
    19→
    20→- [x] **INF-01**: Abstract InferenceProvider interface with generate(), load_adapter(), unload_adapter(), list_adapters() methods
    21→- [x] **INF-02**: VLLMProvider implementation with full LoRA hot-loading support (POST /v1/load_lora_adapter, /v1/unload_lora_adapter)
    22→- [x] **INF-03**: OllamaProvider implementation for inference via Ollama API (LoRA support where Ollama supports it)
    23→- [x] **INF-04**: Provider factory/registry for selecting backend by configuration (env var or config file)
    24→- [x] **INF-05**: User can generate with a specific loaded adapter by passing adapter name as model parameter
    25→- [x] **INF-06**: User can load multiple adapters simultaneously for composition (provider-dependent, graceful degradation)
    26→- [x] **INF-07**: Per-step model/provider configuration — agent can use different models or providers for different steps (e.g., one model for generation, another for reflection)
    27→
    28→### Agent Loop
    29→
    30→- [x] **AGENT-01**: generate_node calls InferenceProvider.generate() with task description and optional adapter, returning generated code (backend-agnostic)
    31→- [x] **AGENT-02**: execute_node runs generated code in a sandboxed subprocess with timeout, returning stdout/stderr/exit_code/tests_passed
    32→- [x] **AGENT-03**: reflect_node accumulates trajectory data (attempt count, code, results) without LLM call
    33→- [x] **AGENT-04**: save_trajectory_node persists trajectory via record_trajectory() and sets outcome
    34→- [x] **AGENT-05**: RuneState includes session_id field for trajectory persistence
    35→- [x] **AGENT-06**: Agent loop closes end-to-end: generate → execute → reflect → retry/save with should_retry routing
    36→
    37→### Training Pipeline
    38→
    39→- [x] **TRAIN-01**: User can record a coding trajectory as structured JSON via record_trajectory(session_id, steps, outcome)
    40→- [x] **TRAIN-02**: User can convert trajectory data to SFT chat format via format_for_sft()
    41→- [ ] **TRAIN-03**: User can create a QLoRA config with NF4 quantization and bfloat16 compute dtype via build_qlora_config()
    42→- [ ] **TRAIN-04**: User can apply a LoRA adapter to a base model via apply_lora_adapter()
    43→- [ ] **TRAIN-05**: QLoRA training pipeline runs end-to-end: trajectory → SFT format → PEFT train → save safetensors → store in registry
    44→- [ ] **TRAIN-06**: training-svc exposes POST /train/lora endpoint with async background job tracking
    45→- [ ] **TRAIN-07**: training-svc pyproject.toml declares model-training as workspace dependency
    46→
    47→### Doc-to-LoRA Hypernetwork
    48→
    49→- [ ] **DTOL-01**: DocToLoraHypernetwork module exists in model-training lib with Perceiver-based architecture
    50→- [ ] **DTOL-02**: Hypernetwork generates rank-8 LoRA adapter weights from coding trajectory in a single forward pass (<1s)
    51→- [ ] **DTOL-03**: Generated adapters are compatible with vLLM dynamic LoRA loading (standard PEFT safetensors format)
    52→- [ ] **DTOL-04**: training-svc exposes POST /train/hypernetwork endpoint for Doc-to-LoRA training
    53→
    54→### Evaluation & Kill-Switch
    55→
    56→- [ ] **EVAL-01**: User can run a HumanEval subset benchmark via run_humaneval_subset()
    57→- [ ] **EVAL-02**: User can calculate Pass@k metrics via calculate_pass_at_k()
    58→- [ ] **EVAL-03**: Kill-switch gate compares baseline vs adapter-enhanced Pass@1 (5% improvement threshold)
    59→
    60→### Infrastructure
    61→
    62→- [x] **INFRA-01**: lora-server Dockerfile uses vllm/vllm-openai:v0.16.0 base image (not python:3.12-slim)
    63→- [x] **INFRA-02**: lora-server sets VLLM_ALLOW_RUNTIME_LORA_UPDATING=True environment variable
    64→- [x] **INFRA-03**: docker-compose resolves port conflict (api-service and lora-server on different host ports)
    65→- [ ] **INFRA-04**: model-training pyproject.toml adds GPU dependencies (peft, bitsandbytes, transformers, trl, datasets) with TYPE_CHECKING guards
    66→- [ ] **INFRA-05**: All GPU imports deferred inside function bodies (not top-level) for CPU-only CI compatibility
    67→
    68→## Future Requirements
    69→
    70→Deferred to v6+. Tracked but not in current roadmap.
    71→
    72→### Evolution
    73→
    74→- **EVOL-01**: Evolution operator scores adapter fitness based on HumanEval pass rate
    75→- **EVOL-02**: Evolution operator performs adapter crossover via weight interpolation
    76→- **EVOL-03**: Evolution operator archives low-fitness adapters
    77→
    78→### Advanced Features
    79→
    80→- **ADV-01**: Embedding-based automatic adapter selection via vector similarity search
    81→- **ADV-02**: HITL UI with session monitoring, adapter library browser, trajectory viewer
    82→- **ADV-03**: Docker container isolation for code execution (replace subprocess sandbox)
    83→
    84→## Out of Scope
    85→
    86→Explicitly excluded. Documented to prevent scope creep.
    87→
    88→| Feature | Reason |
    89→|---------|--------|
    90→| Hardware validation phase | User deferred — will validate hardware separately |
    91→| Tensor parallelism (TP=2) | vLLM bug #21471 — corrupted output on PCIe GPUs without NVLink |
    92→| Adapter merging into base weights | Loses hot-swap composability; vLLM Punica kernels handle LoRA with near-zero overhead |
    93→| Cloud API inference | Hard constraint: local-first, no cloud dependencies for inference. Provider abstraction supports local backends (vLLM, Ollama) only |
    94→| Concurrent training + inference | CUDA OOM — must schedule sequentially |
    95→| Streaming responses in agent loop | Agent needs complete code, not token stream; add to API layer separately if needed |
    96→| Multi-tenant isolation | Single-user local system; session_id provides sufficient organization |
    97→| evolution-svc implementation | Depends on populated adapter library from multiple sessions; v6+ |

1→# Phase 21: QLoRA Training Pipeline - Research
     2→
     3→**Researched:** 2026-03-05
     4→**Domain:** QLoRA fine-tuning (PEFT + TRL + BitsAndBytes), FastAPI background jobs, PEFT safetensors adapter format
     5→**Confidence:** HIGH

    75→
    76→## Summary
    77→
    78→Phase 21 implements the end-to-end QLoRA training pipeline. The key libraries are PEFT (LoraConfig, get_peft_model), BitsAndBytes (BitsAndBytesConfig for 4-bit NF4 quantization), TRL (SFTTrainer + SFTConfig), transformers (AutoModelForCausalLM, AutoTokenizer), and datasets (Dataset.from_list). The work splits across two implementation sites: the model-training library (peft_utils.py, config.py) and the training-svc service (router, job tracking, orchestrator).
    79→
    80→The biggest technical pitfall is the vLLM adapter format constraint: if `modules_to_save` is used in LoraConfig (to save embed_tokens or lm_head), vLLM cannot load the resulting adapter. The solution is to NOT use `modules_to_save` — target only q_proj and v_proj, which produces a clean safetensors file with only lora_A and lora_B weight keys. The confirmed locked decision (target_modules=["q_proj", "v_proj"]) already avoids this pitfall.
    81→
    82→The SFTTrainer integration is straightforward: pass `peft_config=LoraConfig(...)` and the `messages` field in dataset dicts (matching format_for_sft() output). When peft_config is provided, SFTTrainer automatically handles quantized model loading, adapter attachment, and saves only adapter weights (not the full model) on trainer.save_model().
    83→
    84→**Primary recommendation:** Implement `train_qlora()` as a single orchestrator function in a new `model_training/trainer.py` module. This keeps the endpoint handler thin, enables isolated unit testing of the orchestrator, and matches the Phase 19-20 pattern of service-level thin routers delegating to library functions.

    88→## Standard Stack
    89→
    90→### Core
    91→| Library | Version | Purpose | Why Standard |
    92→|---------|---------|---------|--------------|
    93→| peft | >=0.18.0 | LoraConfig, get_peft_model, PeftModel | HuggingFace PEFT is the standard LoRA library; SFTTrainer integrates natively |
    94→| bitsandbytes | >=0.45.0 | BitsAndBytesConfig for NF4 4-bit quantization | Only library providing QLoRA 4-bit NF4 quantization for PyTorch on NVIDIA GPUs |
    95→| transformers | >=4.47.0 | AutoModelForCausalLM, AutoTokenizer, prepare_model_for_kbit_training | Backbone model loading with quantization_config support |
    96→| trl | >=0.16.0 | SFTTrainer, SFTConfig | HuggingFace supervised fine-tuning trainer; native PEFT peft_config parameter |
    97→| datasets | >=3.0.0 | Dataset.from_list() | HuggingFace datasets; Dataset.from_list wraps list[dict] directly |
    98→
    99→### Supporting
   100→| Library | Version | Purpose | When to Use |
   101→|---------|---------|---------|-------------|
   102→| torch | >=2.0.0 (already in pyproject) | Tensor operations, bfloat16 dtype | Already declared; needed for BitsAndBytesConfig dtype constant |
   103→| hashlib | stdlib | SHA-256 hash of adapter file | For AdapterRecord.file_hash field |
   104→| uuid | stdlib | Generate adapter_id | Standard for unique job/adapter IDs |
   105→| dataclasses | stdlib | JobStatus dataclass for in-memory tracking | Clean typed status object without SQLModel overhead |
   106→| tempfile | stdlib | Temp training output directory | SFTTrainer requires output_dir; temp dir is cleaned up after save_pretrained |
   107→| shutil | stdlib | Copy final adapter files from temp dir | Move trained weights to ~/.rune/adapters/{adapter_id}/ |
   108→
   109→### Alternatives Considered
   110→| Instead of | Could Use | Tradeoff |
   111→|------------|-----------|----------|
   112→| FastAPI BackgroundTasks | ARQ/Celery | Celery adds Redis dependency — wrong for single-user local MVP; BackgroundTasks sufficient |

   114→| SFTTrainer | HuggingFace Trainer directly | SFTTrainer wraps Trainer with chat template handling, peft_config integration, and completion-only loss — saves manual setup |

   128→## Architecture Patterns
   129→
   130→### Recommended Project Structure
   131→
   132→New files to create:
   133→
   134→```
   135→libs/model-training/src/model_training/
   136→├── config.py           # EXISTING stub — implement get_training_config() + validate_config()
   137→├── peft_utils.py       # EXISTING stub — implement build_qlora_config() + apply_lora_adapter()
   138→├── trainer.py          # NEW — train_qlora() orchestrator function
   139→└── trajectory.py       # EXISTING — format_for_sft() already done
   140→
   141→services/training-svc/src/training_svc/
   142→├── routers/
   143→│   └── training.py     # EXISTING stub — implement POST /train/lora + GET /jobs/{job_id}
   144→├── jobs.py             # NEW — JobStatus dataclass + module-level JOB_STORE dict
   145→└── schemas.py          # EXISTING — LoraTrainingRequest needs session_id field added
   146→```
   147→
   148→### Pattern 1: GPU Import Deferral (INFRA-04/05)
   149→
   150→**What:** All imports from peft, bitsandbytes, transformers, trl are placed inside function bodies — not at module top-level. TYPE_CHECKING guard wraps type annotations only.
   151→
   152→**When to use:** Every function in model_training that touches a GPU library.
   153→
   154→**Example (from Phase 19-20 established pattern):**
   155→```python
   156→# Source: Phase 19-20 project pattern (model_training/peft_utils.py)
   157→from __future__ import annotations
   158→
   159→from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any
   160→
   161→if TYPE_CHECKING:
   162→    from peft import LoraConfig  # annotation-only, never executed at runtime
   163→
   164→
   165→def build_qlora_config(
   166→    rank: int,
   167→    alpha: int,
   168→    target_modules: list[str],
   169→    dropout: float = 0.1,
   170→) -> Any:  # Any at runtime; LoraConfig annotation only used by type-checkers
   171→    """Build a QLoRA LoraConfig with NF4 quantization settings."""
   172→    from peft import LoraConfig                                  # deferred import
   173→    from transformers import BitsAndBytesConfig                  # deferred import
   174→    import torch                                                  # deferred import
   175→
   176→    bnb_config = BitsAndBytesConfig(
   177→        load_in_4bit=True,
   178→        bnb_4bit_quant_type="nf4",
   179→        bnb_4bit_compute_dtype=torch.bfloat16,
   180→        bnb_4bit_use_double_quant=True,
   181→    )
   182→    return LoraConfig(
   183→        r=rank,
   184→        lora_alpha=alpha,
   185→        target_modules=target_modules,
   186→        lora_dropout=dropout,
   187→        bias="none",
   188→        task_type="CAUSAL_LM",
   189→    )
   190→```
   191→
   192→Note: `BitsAndBytesConfig` lives in `transformers`, not `bitsandbytes` directly. The quantization config is passed to `AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained()`, not embedded in LoraConfig.
   193→
   194→### Pattern 2: SFTTrainer QLoRA Pipeline
   195→
   196→**What:** Load quantized model → attach LoRA via peft_config → train with SFTTrainer → save adapter only.
   197→
   198→**When to use:** In `train_qlora()` orchestrator in `model_training/trainer.py`.
   199→
   200→**Example:**
   201→```python
   202→# Source: TRL official docs (https://huggingface.co/docs/trl/en/sft_trainer)
   203→# and PEFT docs (https://huggingface.co/docs/peft/package_reference/lora)
   204→def train_qlora(
   205→    session_id: str,
   206→    output_dir: str,
   207→    rank: int = 64,
   208→    alpha: int = 128,
   209→    epochs: int = 3,
   210→    learning_rate: float = 2e-4,
   211→) -> str:
   212→    """Train QLoRA adapter and return path to saved adapter directory."""
   213→    import tempfile
   214→    import torch
   215→    from datasets import Dataset
   216→    from transformers import AutoModelForCausalLM, AutoTokenizer, BitsAndBytesConfig
   217→    from trl import SFTConfig, SFTTrainer
   218→
   219→    from model_training.peft_utils import build_qlora_config
   220→    from model_training.trajectory import format_for_sft, load_trajectory
   221→
   222→    # 1. Load and format trajectory
   223→    trajectory = load_trajectory(session_id)
   224→    messages = format_for_sft(trajectory)  # returns [] if not successful
   225→    if not messages:
   226→        raise ValueError(f"Trajectory {session_id} is not successful or has no messages")
   227→
   228→    # 2. Wrap in HuggingFace Dataset with "messages" field
   229→    dataset = Dataset.from_list([{"messages": messages}])
   230→
   231→    # 3. Build quantization config
   232→    bnb_config = BitsAndBytesConfig(
   233→        load_in_4bit=True,
   234→        bnb_4bit_quant_type="nf4",
   235→        bnb_4bit_compute_dtype=torch.bfloat16,
   236→        bnb_4bit_use_double_quant=True,
   237→    )
   238→
   239→    # 4. Load base model with 4-bit quantization
   240→    base_model_id = os.environ.get("RUNE_BASE_MODEL", "Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B")
   241→    model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(
   242→        base_model_id,
   243→        quantization_config=bnb_config,
   244→        device_map="auto",
   245→    )
   246→    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(base_model_id)
   247→
   248→    # 5. Build LoRA config (no modules_to_save — vLLM compatibility)
   249→    lora_config = build_qlora_config(
   250→        rank=rank,
   251→        alpha=alpha,
   252→        target_modules=["q_proj", "v_proj"],
   253→        dropout=0.1,
   254→    )
   255→
   256→    # 6. Train
   257→    training_args = SFTConfig(
   258→        output_dir=output_dir,
   259→        num_train_epochs=epochs,
   260→        learning_rate=learning_rate,
   261→        warmup_ratio=0.03,
   262→        lr_scheduler_type="cosine",
   263→        bf16=True,
   264→        per_device_train_batch_size=1,
   265→        gradient_accumulation_steps=4,
   266→        save_strategy="no",   # no intermediate checkpoints
   267→        logging_steps=1,
   268→    )
   269→    trainer = SFTTrainer(
   270→        model=model,
   271→        args=training_args,
   272→        train_dataset=dataset,
   273→        peft_config=lora_config,
   274→        processing_class=tokenizer,
   275→    )
   276→    trainer.train()
   277→
   278→    # 7. Save adapter only (SFTTrainer saves only adapter when peft_config is set)
   279→    trainer.save_model(output_dir)
   280→    return output_dir
   281→```
   282→
   283→### Pattern 3: FastAPI BackgroundTasks with In-Memory Job Tracking
   284→
   285→**What:** Module-level dict stores JobStatus objects; BackgroundTask updates it during training.
   286→
   287→**When to use:** POST /train/lora endpoint.
   288→
   289→**Example:**
   290→```python
   291→# Source: FastAPI official docs (https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/background-tasks/)
   292→# and project pattern (training-svc/routers/training.py)
   293→import uuid
   294→from dataclasses import dataclass, field
   295→from typing import Optional
   296→
   297→# Module-level in-memory store — lost on restart (acceptable per CONTEXT.md)
   298→JOB_STORE: dict[str, "JobStatus"] = {}
   299→
   300→
   301→@dataclass
   302→class JobStatus:
   303→    job_id: str
   304→    status: str  # "queued" | "running" | "completed" | "failed"
   305→    adapter_id: Optional[str] = None
   306→    error: Optional[str] = None
   307→
   308→
   309→@router.post("/train/lora")
   310→async def train_lora(
   311→    request: LoraTrainingRequest,
   312→    background_tasks: BackgroundTasks,
   313→) -> JSONResponse:
   314→    job_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
   315→    adapter_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
   316→    JOB_STORE[job_id] = JobStatus(job_id=job_id, status="queued")
   317→    background_tasks.add_task(
   318→        _run_training_job,
   319→        job_id=job_id,
   320→        session_id=request.session_id,
   321→        adapter_id=adapter_id,
   322→        rank=request.rank,
   323→        epochs=request.epochs,
   324→    )
   325→    return JSONResponse(content={"job_id": job_id, "status": "queued"})
   326→
   327→
   328→@router.get("/jobs/{job_id}")
   329→async def get_job_status(job_id: str) -> JSONResponse:
   330→    job = JOB_STORE.get(job_id)
   331→    if job is None:
   332→        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f"Job {job_id} not found")
   333→    return JSONResponse(content={
   334→        "job_id": job.job_id,
   335→        "status": job.status,
   336→        "adapter_id": job.adapter_id,
   337→        "error": job.error,
   338→    })
   339→```
   340→
   341→### Pattern 4: AdapterRecord Creation After Training
   342→
   343→**What:** Build AdapterRecord from trained adapter directory metadata and call registry.store().
   344→
   345→**When to use:** At the end of `_run_training_job()` in training-svc.
   346→
   347→**Example:**
   348→```python
   349→# Source: Phase 18 adapter-registry pattern (adapter_registry/models.py)
   350→import hashlib
   351→import os
   352→from datetime import datetime, timezone
   353→from pathlib import Path
   354→
   355→from adapter_registry.models import AdapterRecord
   356→from adapter_registry.registry import AdapterRegistry
   357→
   358→def _build_adapter_record(
   359→    adapter_id: str,
   360→    adapter_dir: Path,
   361→    session_id: str,
   362→    task_type: str,
   363→    rank: int,
   364→    base_model_id: str,
   365→) -> AdapterRecord:
   366→    safetensors_path = adapter_dir / "adapter_model.safetensors"
   367→    file_bytes = safetensors_path.read_bytes()
   368→    file_hash = hashlib.sha256(file_bytes).hexdigest()
   369→    return AdapterRecord(
   370→        id=adapter_id,
   371→        version=1,
   372→        task_type=task_type,
   373→        base_model_id=base_model_id,
   374→        rank=rank,
   375→        created_at=datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc).isoformat(),
   376→        file_path=str(adapter_dir),
   377→        file_hash=file_hash,
   378→        file_size_bytes=safetensors_path.stat().st_size,
   379→        source="qlora",
   380→        session_id=session_id,
   381→    )
   382→```
   383→
   384→### Anti-Patterns to Avoid
   385→
   386→- **Top-level GPU imports:** `import peft` or `import transformers` at module level causes ImportError in CPU-only CI. Always import inside function bodies.
   387→- **`modules_to_save` in LoraConfig:** Adding embed_tokens or lm_head to modules_to_save saves full embedding weights into adapter_model.safetensors. vLLM cannot load adapters that include these keys — raises RuntimeError "Loading lora failed / is unsupported LoRA weight". Do NOT use modules_to_save.
   388→- **Saving the merged model:** Calling `model.merge_and_unload()` then `save_pretrained()` saves the full 7B model (14GB+), not just the adapter (tens of MB). Use `trainer.save_model()` while peft_config is active — this saves only adapter weights.
   389→- **SFTTrainer without `processing_class`:** If tokenizer is not passed, SFTTrainer attempts to load from model name string. When passing a model object (already loaded), explicitly pass `processing_class=tokenizer`.
   390→- **Module-level env var reads in training-svc router:** Follow Phase 19-20 pattern — read env vars inside function bodies for monkeypatch testability.
   391→- **Storing JOB_STORE inside a request handler closure:** The dict must be module-level so all requests share the same store instance. A dict defined inside the handler would be per-request.
   392→- **SFTTrainer dataset without "messages" field:** SFTTrainer auto-detects conversational format from a "messages" key. The format_for_sft() output is list[dict] with role/content keys — must be wrapped as `{"messages": messages_list}` when creating the Dataset.
   393→
   394→---
   395→
   396→## Don't Hand-Roll
   397→
   398→| Problem | Don't Build | Use Instead | Why |
   399→|---------|-------------|-------------|-----|
   400→| 4-bit quantization | Custom quantization code | BitsAndBytesConfig | NF4 has specific double-quant, storage dtype, compute dtype interactions — subtle to get right |
   401→| LoRA weight injection | Custom layer replacement | peft.get_peft_model() | Handles freezing base weights, gradient routing, adapter naming conventions |
   402→| Chat tokenization | Custom chat template application | SFTTrainer with "messages" field | Each model has its own chat template (Qwen2.5 has a predefined template) — SFTTrainer applies it correctly via apply_chat_template |
   403→| Adapter-only saving | Custom state_dict filtering | trainer.save_model() (PEFT-aware) | SFTTrainer detects peft_config is set and calls model.save_pretrained() on the PeftModel, which saves only LoRA weights |
   404→| UUID generation | Custom ID scheme | uuid.uuid4() | Standard, collision-free, no external dependencies |
   405→| File integrity | Custom hashing | hashlib.sha256() | stdlib, well-tested |
   406→| Async job dispatch | Thread pool or asyncio.create_task | FastAPI BackgroundTasks | Integrates with FastAPI lifecycle; sufficient for single-user local MVP |
   407→
   408→**Key insight:** The PEFT + TRL combination was specifically designed to handle the 15+ edge cases in QLoRA training (gradient checkpointing with quantized layers, adapter-only saving, chat template handling). The entire pipeline is 20-30 lines of orchestration code over library calls — any custom implementation will miss edge cases.
   409→
   410→---
   411→
   412→## Common Pitfalls
   413→
   414→### Pitfall 1: modules_to_save Breaks vLLM Loading
   415→**What goes wrong:** Using `modules_to_save=["embed_tokens", "lm_head"]` in LoraConfig saves the full embedding matrix in adapter_model.safetensors. When vLLM attempts to load the adapter, it fails with RuntimeError because it only knows how to handle lora_A/lora_B weight keys.
   416→**Why it happens:** PEFT includes full module weights (not just deltas) in the adapter file when modules_to_save is set.
   417→**How to avoid:** Do NOT set modules_to_save at all. The locked decision (target_modules=["q_proj", "v_proj"]) already avoids this — vLLM loads cleanly.
   418→**Warning signs:** adapter_model.safetensors is unexpectedly large (>1GB instead of a few MB); vLLM logs "Loading lora failed" or "is unsupported LoRA weight".
   419→
   420→### Pitfall 2: SFTTrainer dataset_text_field vs "messages" Field
   421→**What goes wrong:** Passing a dataset with a "text" field containing pre-formatted string instead of a "messages" field. This bypasses chat template application and may produce malformed training examples.
   422→**Why it happens:** SFTTrainer has two dataset mode auto-detection paths: "messages" for conversational (applies chat template) and "text" for pre-tokenized text.
   423→**How to avoid:** Wrap format_for_sft() output as `{"messages": messages_list}` — exactly matching the "conversational" dataset format. SFTTrainer will apply Qwen2.5's built-in chat template automatically.
   424→**Warning signs:** Training loss starts very low (model already knows the format) or very high (format mismatch).
   425→
   426→### Pitfall 3: GPU Import at Test-Time in CPU-Only CI
   427→**What goes wrong:** Any `import peft`, `import bitsandbytes`, `import transformers`, or `import trl` at module top-level causes ImportError when the GPU packages are not installed (CPU CI).
   428→**Why it happens:** These packages have binary extensions that may not be installed in CPU test environments.
   429→**How to avoid:** All GPU imports inside function bodies only. TYPE_CHECKING guard for type annotations. `model_training/__init__.py` must NOT import from peft_utils or trainer directly (or at minimum those imports must also be deferred).
   430→**Warning signs:** `import model_training` raises ImportError or ModuleNotFoundError in CI logs.
   431→
   432→### Pitfall 4: BackgroundTasks Race Condition on JOB_STORE
   433→**What goes wrong:** The background task starts updating JOB_STORE before the dict entry is created, causing a KeyError or status appearing as "queued" when it's actually failed.
   434→**Why it happens:** BackgroundTasks runs after response is sent; if the dict entry isn't created before add_task(), the task may run before the endpoint sets initial status.
   435→**How to avoid:** Create the JOB_STORE entry with status="queued" BEFORE calling background_tasks.add_task(). The endpoint returns the job_id immediately; the task reads/writes the same entry.
   436→**Warning signs:** GET /jobs/{job_id} returns 404 for a job that was just created.
   437→
   438→### Pitfall 5: SFTTrainer output_dir Checkpoint Pollution
   439→**What goes wrong:** SFTTrainer with save_strategy="steps" (default) creates checkpoint-N subdirectories during training. After training, the adapter files may be in a checkpoint subdirectory, not the root output_dir.
   440→**Why it happens:** save_strategy defaults to "steps" and saves checkpoints into output_dir/checkpoint-N/.
   441→**How to avoid:** Set `save_strategy="no"` in SFTConfig to disable intermediate checkpoints, then explicitly call `trainer.save_model(output_dir)` after training completes. This puts adapter_model.safetensors and adapter_config.json directly in output_dir.
   442→**Warning signs:** output_dir contains checkpoint-N/ subdirectories but no adapter_model.safetensors at the root.
   443→
   444→### Pitfall 6: LoraTrainingRequest Missing session_id Field
   445→**What goes wrong:** The existing LoraTrainingRequest schema has task_type, adapter_id, rank, epochs — but NOT session_id. The endpoint cannot find the trajectory without a session_id.
   446→**Why it happens:** The stub was created before the training pipeline design was finalized.
   447→**How to avoid:** Add `session_id: str` to LoraTrainingRequest in schemas.py. This is a schema change that requires updating the existing xfail test.
   448→**Warning signs:** POST /train/lora has no way to identify which trajectory to train on.

   452→## Code Examples
   453→
   454→Verified patterns from official sources:
   455→
   456→### BitsAndBytesConfig (4-bit NF4 QLoRA)
   457→```python
   458→# Source: https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/en/quantization/bitsandbytes
   459→# and TRL SFT docs: https://huggingface.co/docs/trl/en/sft_trainer
   460→import torch
   461→from transformers import BitsAndBytesConfig
   462→
   463→bnb_config = BitsAndBytesConfig(
   464→    load_in_4bit=True,
   465→    bnb_4bit_quant_type="nf4",          # NF4 = NormalFloat4, better than int4 for LLM weights
   466→    bnb_4bit_compute_dtype=torch.bfloat16,  # bfloat16 avoids NaN at 7B (float16 is unstable)
   467→    bnb_4bit_use_double_quant=True,      # double quantization reduces memory further
   468→)
   469→```
   470→
   471→### LoraConfig for CAUSAL_LM (vLLM-compatible)
   472→```python
   473→# Source: https://huggingface.co/docs/peft/package_reference/lora#peft.LoraConfig
   474→from peft import LoraConfig
   475→
   476→lora_config = LoraConfig(
   477→    r=64,
   478→    lora_alpha=128,            # alpha = 2 * rank is standard convention
   479→    target_modules=["q_proj", "v_proj"],  # attention projections only, no modules_to_save
   480→    lora_dropout=0.1,
   481→    bias="none",
   482→    task_type="CAUSAL_LM",    # required for decoder-only models (Qwen2.5, LLaMA, etc.)
   483→    # DO NOT add modules_to_save — breaks vLLM loading
   484→)
   485→```
   486→
   487→### Dataset.from_list with "messages" field
   488→```python
   489→# Source: TRL SFT docs dataset formats section
   490→# https://huggingface.co/docs/trl/en/sft_trainer#expected-dataset-type-and-format
   491→from datasets import Dataset
   492→
   493→# format_for_sft() returns: [{"role": "system", ...}, {"role": "user", ...}, {"role": "assistant", ...}]
   494→messages = format_for_sft(trajectory)  # list[dict]
   495→dataset = Dataset.from_list([{"messages": messages}])
   496→# SFTTrainer detects "messages" key → applies Qwen2.5's chat template automatically
   497→```
   498→
   499→### SFTTrainer with peft_config (saves adapter only)
   500→```python
   501→# Source: TRL docs PEFT integration section
   502→# https://huggingface.co/docs/trl/en/sft_trainer#train-adapters-with-peft
   503→from trl import SFTConfig, SFTTrainer
   504→
   505→training_args = SFTConfig(
   506→    output_dir=output_dir,
   507→    num_train_epochs=3,
   508→    learning_rate=2e-4,
   509→    warmup_ratio=0.03,
   510→    lr_scheduler_type="cosine",
   511→    bf16=True,
   512→    per_device_train_batch_size=1,
   513→    gradient_accumulation_steps=4,
   514→    save_strategy="no",   # disable intermediate checkpoints
   515→    logging_steps=1,
   516→    report_to="none",     # disable wandb/tensorboard in local training
   517→)
   518→trainer = SFTTrainer(
   519→    model=model,                    # pre-loaded quantized model OR model name string
   520→    args=training_args,
   521→    train_dataset=dataset,
   522→    peft_config=lora_config,        # triggers adapter-only training and saving
   523→    processing_class=tokenizer,
   524→)
   525→trainer.train()
   526→trainer.save_model(output_dir)      # saves adapter_model.safetensors + adapter_config.json ONLY
   527→```
   528→
   529→### apply_lora_adapter via get_peft_model
   530→```python
   531→# Source: https://huggingface.co/docs/peft/package_reference/lora#peft.LoraModel
   532→from peft import get_peft_model
   533→
   534→def apply_lora_adapter(model, config):
   535→    """Wrap base model with LoRA adapter for training."""
   536→    from peft import get_peft_model  # deferred import
   537→    return get_peft_model(model, config)
   538→```
   539→
   540→Note: When using SFTTrainer with peft_config, get_peft_model is called internally. apply_lora_adapter is more useful for inference-time adapter loading or when using the PEFT model outside SFTTrainer.
